title: viola × cracoviensis
intro: Viola × cracoviensis, also known as the Cracow violet or violet, is a hybrid species of flowering plant that belongs to the Viola genus. The medicinal properties of Viola × cracoviensis include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of menopause, and support immune function, among others. Herbal preparations of Viola × cracoviensis often involve using the leaves and flowers to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
